subject: Remove go.sum file
commit_message:
Remove go.sum file

The go.sum file stores checksum values for go modules used for this
project. Some values are not the same across different platforms.
This change removes this file and update the go.mod file with the
versions that are working in Linux and MacOS platforms.

Change-Id: I6245001952097e8b2521e336228270cf6e4bfffd
Signed-off-by: Victor Morales <victor.morales@intel.com>
Issue-ID: MULTICLOUD-392

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Removing go.sum removes exact replication.
What is being built on another machine maybe different than what is getting built in my environment.
go.sum has checksums that ensure that the exact commit is being used for the build.
I don't think removing this file is the solution here.

Patch Set 3:

> Removing go.sum removes exact replication.
 > What is being built on another machine maybe different than what is
 > getting built in my environment.
 > go.sum has checksums that ensure that the exact commit is being
 > used for the build.
 > I don't think removing this file is the solution here.

Agree, the only problem that I have is with these two modules that their hashcodes are not working for MacOS,

k8s.io/client-go v10.0.0+incompatible
github.com/hashicorp/go-rootcerts v1.0.0 // indirect

I can keep those differences locally.

Patch Set 3:

> Agree, the only problem that I have is with these two modules that
 > their hashcodes are not working for MacOS,
 > 
 > k8s.io/client-go v10.0.0+incompatible
 > github.com/hashicorp/go-rootcerts v1.0.0 // indirect
 > 
 > I can keep those differences locally.

Interesting that the client-go for the recommended version does not seem to work.
Since, our target platform is supposed to be linux, I think it is fine to keep those
differences locally for now.

Abandoned

MacOS mismatches can be managed locally
